topStories.title: Melania Trump calls for AI in education as US' student scores hit 20-year low: 35% 12th graders proficient in reading
topStories.description: Citing NAEP's 2024 national sampling, Melania Trump said that USA's average scores have reached their lowest levels in 20 years

topStories.title: Nixon to Trump: Pakistan’s long record as backchannel between rival powers
topStories.description: Islamabad reprises its role as diplomatic broker as US and Iran edge closer to wider confrontation.

topStories.title: Norway to add more than $11bn to defence budget over 10 years
topStories.description: Norway, as well as other NATO countries, has been under pressure from the US to boost defence spending.

topStories.title: US-based burger chain awarded  ₹15 crore in bonuses to employees under pressure
topStories.description: The demand was so high that several outlets suffered severe shortages and were even compelled to close early. Despite these challenges, employees managed long queues and handled the intense pressure well.

topStories.title: "Alarmingly Low": Pentagon Scrambles After US Fires 850 Tomahawks At Iran
topStories.description: It can take up to 2 years to build a Tomahawk, costing $3.6 million a piece, according to the report. Moreover, last year's budget had included only 57 of them.

dailyNews.title: White House launches new app for real-time updates, live streams ‘straight from the source’ - Details here
dailyNews.description: White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt called the app ‘incredible’ and Director of Communications Steven Cheung teased Easter Eggs ‘sprinkled throughout’

dailyNews.title: Congress terms price reduction as a ploy to help oil firms and cautions of a hike after polls
Opposition’s notice seeking removal of Chief Election Commissioner still unacknowledged: Derek O’Brien
dailyNews.description: Senior Trinamool Congress leader alleges ‘tacit understanding’ between Centre and Election Commission on the matter

dailyNews.title: US-Iran war: Egypt's restaurants, malls to partially close amid energy woes — will it impact tourists?
dailyNews.description: Middle-East crisis: Egypt Prime Minister Mostafa Madbouly said shops, shopping centres, restaurants and cafes will all close at 9:00 pm on weekdays.

dailyNews.title: United States only confident that about one-third of Iran’s missile arsenal, drones have been destroyed so far
dailyNews.description: Despite US-Israeli attacks aimed at crippling Iran's missile arsenal, the country continues missile launches daily. Estimates reveal only a third of missiles confirmed destroyed, while Iran asserts ongoing production and capability, posing potential risks to US interests.

dailyNews.title: Karnataka bypolls: Congress rebel candidate retires from contest in Davangere South
dailyNews.description: Rebel candidate Sadiq Pahilwan announced that he would extend full support to Congress candidate Samarth Mallikarjun, son of Minister S.S. Mallikarjun

dailyNews.title: US Court Stops Pentagon From Blacklisting AI Company Anthropic
dailyNews.description: The dispute reportedly began after disagreements between the company and the US Department of Defense over how its AI technology should be used.

dailyNews.title: Trump's paused attack on Iran's energy - for diplomacy or escalation?
dailyNews.description: The US president's commitment to deadlines is fluid but he uses them for a purpose, writes the BBC's James Landale.

dailyNews.title: Qatari PM and US officials discuss strategic ties amid Iran war
dailyNews.description: The meeting reviewed the 'close strategic cooperation' between Doha and Washington, Qatar's foreign ministry said.

dailyNews.title: Iran expert says US ground invasion could cause a global economic meltdown
dailyNews.description: Ali Vaez says the US and Iran have reached a stalemate where further escalation would likely lead to catastrophe.

dailyNews.title: BBC journalist caught in travel chaos at Houston Airport
dailyNews.description: The airport is experiencing some of the longest delays in the US as TSA agents work without pay in a partial shutdown.

dailyNews.title: Two Cuba-bound aid ships missing after leaving Mexico
dailyNews.description: Mexico's navy launches a rescue mission for ships delivering aid to Cuba, strained by food and fuel shortages amid a US embargo.

dailyNews.title: Guardians rookie has historic two-homer performance in regular season debut: 'He's not from this planet'
dailyNews.description: Cleveland Guardians rookie Chase DeLauter homered twice in his regular-season debut, becoming just the sixth player in MLB history to accomplish the feat.

dailyNews.title: Illegal immigrant accused of killing Chicago college student to face court after tuberculosis delay
dailyNews.description: Jose Medina-Medina, accused of killing Loyola University Chicago student Sheridan Gorman, faces his first court hearing after a tuberculosis delay.

dailyNews.title: ‘Lovers’ Lane’ murders suspect nabbed decades after couple found dead in car
dailyNews.description: Floyd William Parrott is charged with capital murder in the 1990 'Lovers' Lane' cold case killings of two Texas victims after 36 years unsolved.

dailyNews.title: Valerie Bertinelli’s body confession: Why the star says she is now ‘deformed’
dailyNews.description: Valerie Bertinelli opens up to Drew Barrymore about her memoir "Getting Naked," revealing four surgeries in 2024 and candid thoughts on her body image.

dailyNews.title: Spanberger takes fire from all sides as Youngkin, Angel parent slams recent controversies
dailyNews.description: Ex-Gov. Youngkin calls Spanberger's redistricting support a 'blatant lie,' while an Angel Mom challenges her over an illegal immigrant accused of murder.

dailyNews.title: Vance says Biden admin 'turned off' anti-fraud protections, debuts new task force with focus on Somali schemes
dailyNews.description: Vice President JD Vance claims the Biden administration turned off anti-fraud protections, leading the first task force meeting on Medicare and Medicaid fraud.

topStories.title: Trump’s Signature Is Set to Be Added to America’s Currency
topStories.description: President Trump is poised to be the first sitting president to have his signature appear on the U.S. dollar.

topStories.title: U.S. May Label Brazilian Gangs as Terror Groups, After Push by the Bolsonaros
topStories.description: The Trump administration is weighing declaring Brazil’s two largest drug gangs terrorist groups, after lobbying by the sons of jailed former President Jair Bolsonaro.

topStories.title: Standoff With Iran Raises Fresh Doubts About Trump’s Freestyle Diplomacy
topStories.description: A jumble of emissaries — a friend, a family member, a dove and a hawk — on the Iran crisis reflects President Trump’s improvisational approach.

topStories.title: One Republican Farmer in Wisconsin Is Bitter Over Trump’s Deportations
topStories.description: For 20 years, Tim O’Harrow has asked lawmakers to acknowledge that America’s food supply depends on immigrants. Politics went in the other direction.

topStories.title: Europeans Are Angry at Trump, but Often Forgiving of Americans
topStories.description: A generation ago, foreign fury over the Iraq invasion often blurred into anti-Americanism. Now, some Europeans seem ready to distinguish between the president and the American people.

topStories.title: ‘No Kings’ Protests Across U.S. Cities: What We Know
topStories.description: Thousands of demonstrations against the Trump administration are scheduled to take place in cities and towns across the country on Saturday.

topStories.title: Iran war rages, Strait of Hormuz still blocked as Trump pushes for a deal
topStories.description: The Trump administration expects Iran's formal response to its 15-point peace proposal today, as Tehran continues blocking the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: Iran war rages, Strait of Hormuz still blocked as Trump pushes for a deal
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration expects Iran's formal response to its 15-point peace proposal today, as Tehran continues blocking the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: Could Trump suspend the federal gas tax as the Iran war continues?
dailyNews.description: President Trump said he could consider suspending the federal gas tax as the conflict in the Middle East continues. Barron's reporter Jacob Sonenshine joins CBS News with more.

dailyNews.title: Israel says it will continue to "hammer" Iran even as Trump seeks deal with Tehran
dailyNews.description: Danny Danon, Israel's ambassador to the United Nations, says America's top ally in the Middle East will continue to strike Iran despite U.S. efforts to negotiate with Tehran.

dailyNews.title: Iran maintains chokehold on Strait of Hormuz despite Trump's deadlines
dailyNews.description: The Iran war is nearing the one-month mark as different versions of the state of apparent negotiations between Tehran and the U.S. emerge. CBS News' Chris Livesay reports.

dailyNews.title: Senate approves most DHS funding, hours after Trump promised to restart TSA pay
dailyNews.description: The Senate agreed early Friday to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security in an effort to end a standoff in Congress that led to massive lines at many airports.

dailyNews.title: Brent oil tops $110 again after Chinese ships are turned away from Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: Oil prices traded higher after U.S. President Donald Trump said Iran had allowed 10 oil tankers to pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: David Sacks says his time as Trump's crypto and AI czar has ended
dailyNews.description: The venture capitalist said he will still be a part of the White House's Technology committee and will help push Trump's AI plan forward.

dailyNews.title: Anthropic wins preliminary injunction in DOD fight as judge cites 'First Amendment retaliation'
dailyNews.description: A federal judge in San Francisco granted Anthropic's request for a preliminary injunction in its lawsuit against the Trump administration.

dailyNews.title: Trump Delays Strikes on Iran's Energy Sector for 10 More Days
dailyNews.description: After nearly one month of war, the United States, Israel and Iran are continuing to trade air and drone strikes. Now President Donald Trump says they’re also trading potential peace plans but Iran is continuing to publicly dispute that any talks are underway. It comes as the president is extending a deadline he gave Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz of face attacks against its power plants. NBC’s Garrett Haake reports for TODAY from the White House.

dailyNews.title: Ambitious, authoritarian and talking to Trump? The hard-liners rising in Iran
dailyNews.description: The Israeli military has systematically killed Iran’s top leaders, so when President Donald Trump said this week that talks are being held with Iran, the biggest question was: Who exactly is the U.S. talking to

dailyNews.title: Covid and flu shots in limbo as Trump administration mulls appeal over vaccine panel
dailyNews.description: After a federal judge blocked Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s vaccine policies, the Justice Department has yet to appeal the decision — and ultimately may not.

dailyNews.title: Iran live updates: More than 300 American troops wounded in Iran war
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump announced "major combat operations" against Iran on Feb. 28, with massive joint U.S.-Israeli strikes.

dailyNews.title: WATCH:  Melania Trump showcases robot guest at WH summit
dailyNews.description: A walking, talking humanoid robot strolled side-by-side with first lady Melania Trump at the White House for the second day of her international technology summit.
